title: pseudophegopteris keraudreniana
intro: Pseudophegopteris keraudreniana is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves and rhizome of Pseudophegopteris keraudreniana are used to prepare herbal remedies, which may also help in lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including infusions, teas, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
